title: What are the 4 versioning concerns in LLMOps, and what happens when each changes?
prompt:
What are the 4 versioning concerns in LLMOps, and what happens when each changes?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Classical MLOps versions datasets and models. LLMOps has four distinct artifacts requiring strict version control: (1) Model version — the underlying API model (e.g. GPT-4-0409); provider updates silently shift tone, formatting, or safety behavior, so pin versions and run a full regression before routing traffic to a new one. (2) Prompt version — system prompts and few-shot examples; any edit can improve targeted cases while breaking others, so never deploy a prompt change without running the full evaluation set. (3) Retrieval config — embeddings, chunking, and index parameters; changes what context the model sees, altering outputs even if prompts and models stay identical. (4) Evaluation set — test cases and rubrics; changing the test set breaks historical comparability, so always document changes and keep old sets around for continuity.

**Takeaway:** All four artifacts move independently and each can silently change system behavior on its own — treat every one of them as a versioned, reviewable artifact, not just the model.
hint_notes: Model, prompt, retrieval config, and eval set are 4 independent versioning axes — a regression can come from any one changing while the others stay fixed.
